chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA C.P.No.D-623 of 2024 Date ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on M.A.No.2086/2025 (U/A). 2. For orders on office objections at flag "A". 3. For orders on M.A.No.2087/2025 (E/A). 4. For hearing of main case. ------ 03.07.2025 Mr. Mumtaz Ali Brohi, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Liaquat Ali Shar, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. ---------------- Through this petition, the petitioner is seeking directions to Respondents No.1 to 3 (Revenue Officials) to proceed with and dispose of his application dated 18.6.2025, filed for carrying out the measurement and demarcation of the land bearing Survey No.827, situated in Deh Talbani, Tapo Abra, Taluka and District Larkana. Learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh, present in the Court in some other matters, waives notice of instant petition and submits that recently, the Senior Member Board of Revenue, Sindh, through Notification No.DD(E&I)/HQ/BOR/2024/302, dated 21.10.2024 has established a "Public Grievances Redressal" under the aegis of the concerned Deputy Commissioner across Sindh. This cell is mandated to address public grievances pertaining to revenue matters, including mutation entries, sale certificates, foti-khata-badal, demarcation of land, land partition, and solvency certificates. The establishment of this cell provides an alternate, effective, and exhaustive remedy for redressal of complaints related to revenue issues. Accordingly, instant petition is disposed of, leaving it open for the petitioner to avail the remedy delineated by the “Public Grievances Redressal Cell" as prescribed by the Notification.

The establishment of this cell provides an alternate, effective, and exhaustive remedy for redressal of complaints related to revenue issues. Accordingly, instant petition is disposed of, leaving it open for the petitioner to avail the remedy delineated by the “Public Grievances Redressal Cell" as prescribed by the Notification. The Public Grievance Redressal Cell is hereby directed to decide the application of the petitioner, if any, within two months’ time in accordance with law. JUDGE JUDGE S.Ashfaq/-
